goal: Recall is reliable across long histories, not just keyword-grep over recent messages.
why: autoRecall greps chat_messages for >=5-char word overlaps and recallMemory does ilike %query% (memoryTools.ts:20-100) -- both miss paraphrases and drown in long histories. Replace substring recall with memory formation (extracted facts) + a 3-signal hybrid ranker per ADR-002, preserving the dual-path model and REQ-3 principal isolation.
success_criteria: Conversations are reduced to durable extracted facts (memory_facts), embedded and principal-scoped, that the agent recalls -- not raw message substrings.
Recall ranks by salience + recency (cosine + lexical + recency-decay + importance) and surfaces a paraphrased fact an ilike keyword match would miss.
The dual-path model is preserved: both autoRecall and recall_memory route through hybridRecall; the recall_memory tool input/output contract is unchanged and onedriveTools.test.ts still passes.
Memory is scoped to the session principal: every memory_facts query filters on principal; cross-principal recall returns nothing (REQ-3).
Fact extraction is idempotent (upsert on (session_id, fact)), runs on session close and via a 5-min cron sweep, with per-session error isolation.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0 and npx vitest run passes (existing + new suites).
Migration 0009 enables pgvector and creates memory_facts with RLS on, no policies, and a principal check constraint.

tasks.title: Migration 0009: pgvector + memory_facts table
acceptance_criteria: supabase/migrations/0009_memory_facts.sql exists, numbered after 0008.
Migration runs 'create extension if not exists vector;' to enable pgvector.
Creates public.memory_facts with principal check (Wency, Jeanette), session_id, fact, summary, embedding vector(768), importance int 1..5, created_at.
Unique constraint memory_facts_session_fact_uniq (session_id, fact) supports idempotent upsert.
RLS enabled with NO policies; principal check constraint present.
Btree index on (principal, created_at) and a cosine ANN index on embedding.
tasks.action: Mirror 0004_chat_messages.sql and 0007_scheduled_emails.sql comment/RLS pattern. Enable pgvector, create memory_facts with the listed columns/constraints/indexes, add the (session_id, fact) unique constraint, enable RLS with no policies, use vector(768) to match the adapter output_dimensionality.

verification.pattern: create extension if not exists vector

tasks.title: Embedding adapter (src/lib/embeddings/) + env block
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/embeddings/index.ts exports embedText(text): Promise<number[]> returning a 768-length vector.
Provider resolved by config (GOOGLE_API_KEY -> Gemini gemini-embedding-001 embedContent), model overridable via EMBEDDING_MODEL, dim via config -- never hardcoded in callers.
src/lib/env.ts gains lazy getEmbeddingsEnv() following the getTavilyEnv/getCryptoEnv pattern.
Adapter unit-testable without a network call: test mocks fetch, asserts returned vector and correct request shape (embedContent URL, content.parts[0].text).
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; new test passes.
tasks.action: Mirror client.ts:182-239 Gemini-via-env resolution and env.ts:95-126 lazy-cached Zod pattern. Implement embedText posting to the Gemini models/${model}:embedContent endpoint with x-goog-api-key header and body { content: { parts: [{ text }] }, output_dimensionality: dim }; parse embedding.values; throw on non-2xx or wrong length. Add a vitest stubbing global.fetch.

tasks.title: Memory store: fact extraction + hybrid ranker + rewire both recall paths
acceptance_criteria: memoryStore.ts exports extractFacts (LLM-extract + embed + upsert keyed on (session_id, fact)), rankFacts (pure, deterministic score = w_sim*cosine + w_lex*lexical + w_rec*recencyDecay + w_imp*importanceNorm), hybridRecall (embed query, fetch principal-scoped candidates, rank, top-K).
recallMemory(query, principal) calls hybridRecall and returns the SAME JSON string shape; signature unchanged (AC3).
autoRecall(principal, userText) calls hybridRecall and still returns string | null (AC3).
Every memory_facts query filters .eq('principal', principal) (AC4 / REQ-3).
Existing onedriveTools.test.ts passes unchanged; new memoryStore.test.ts covers AC1 paraphrase, AC2 deterministic order, AC4 isolation, AC5 idempotency.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; npx vitest run passes.
tasks.action: Create memoryStore.ts importing supabaseAdmin and embedText. rankFacts (pure): cosine + >=5-char lexical hit (reuse memoryTools.ts:67-75) + exp(-ageHours/halfLife) recency + importance/5; weights sim.5 lex.2 rec.2 imp.1; ties by created_at. hybridRecall: embed query, select principal-scoped facts, rank, slice. extractFacts: openrouter chat extracts facts+importance, embed, upsert onConflict 'session_id,fact'. Rewire recallMemory+autoRecall to hybridRecall, remove ilike. Add memoryStore.test.ts.

tasks.title: Build cadence: cron sweep route for fact extraction
acceptance_criteria: src/app/api/memory/sweep/route.ts exports a GET handler guarded by CRON_SECRET bearer (exact pattern of mail/scheduled/run/route.ts); 401 without the matching bearer.
Handler finds stragglers: closed sessions (not the principal's latest) with no memory_facts rows, runs extractFacts per session with per-session try/catch (mirrors runDue).
vercel.json gains { path: /api/memory/sweep, schedule: */5 * * * * }; existing mail cron preserved.
Running the sweep twice does not duplicate facts (carried by T3's (session_id, fact) upsert).
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; sweep route test passes (auth guard + straggler selection mocked).
tasks.action: Copy the CRON_SECRET bearer guard and runtime/dynamic exports from mail/scheduled/run/route.ts. Query chat_messages for (principal, session_id) groups, exclude each principal latest session, skip sessions already having a memory_facts row, call extractFacts per remaining session in try/catch tallying processed/failed. Add the sweep cron to vercel.json keeping the mail entry. Add a vitest: 401 without bearer (extractFacts not called); correct bearer over a straggler -> extractFacts called once.
